commit.message:
Generate Unicode category/script tables at runtime; update case fold to Unicode 17.0 (#126)

Replace ~2,000 lines of static Unicode category and script arrays with
runtime generation from JDK's Character.getType() and
Character.UnicodeScript.of(). This ensures categories and scripts always
match the running JDK's Unicode version.

Case folding (CASE_FOLD, TO_LOWER) remains static but is updated to
Unicode 17.0 (CaseFolding.txt), adding entries for Garay, Beria Erfe,
Cyrillic TJE, and several Latin pairs. Static fold tables can safely be
ahead of the JDK — entries for unrecognized code points are harmless
dead code, and Unicode's stability policy prevents reassignment.

Also fixes unicodeSpace() which incorrectly included U+001C-001F (file/
group/record/unit separators) — these are not in Unicode's White_Space
property. Filed as #125.

Adds UnicodeJdkConsistencyTest with canary tests that will fail when a
new JDK ships with a newer Unicode version that adds case pairs or
whitespace characters, signaling that the static tables need updating.

Fixes #107
Refs #125

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: keyboard shortcuts, MCP config refresh, session links, spec accuracy (#125)

* fix: wire keyboard shortcut preferences into runtime bindings

Settings UI already let users remap keyboard shortcuts (stored as
Ctrl+N format in preferences.keyboardShortcuts), but CommandPalette
used hardcoded display-format shortcuts (⌘N) and App.tsx hardcoded
Ctrl+K for the palette toggle. Runtime bindings never consumed the
user's configured preferences.

Changes:
- useKeyboardShortcuts: unified parser handles both settings (Ctrl+X)
  and display (⌘X) formats with full Alt modifier support; exports
  settingsToDisplayShortcut, matchesShortcutConfig, COMMAND_SHORTCUT_MAP
- CommandPalette: reads preferences.keyboardShortcuts to populate each
  command's shortcut dynamically with defensive fallbacks
- App.tsx: palette toggle uses shared matchesShortcutConfig instead of
  duplicated inline parser
- ActivityBar: tooltip shows user-configured palette shortcut
- 19 new tests covering settings format parsing, Alt modifiers,
  display conversion, matchesShortcutConfig, and exact modifier matching

* fix: refresh MCP configs when plugin settings are updated

When plugin settings change via PUT /plugin/{id}/settings, the MCP
config files were not being refreshed — setting variable substitution
in MCP server args/env would remain stale until plugin reload.

Added RefreshMcpConfigsAsync to IPluginHost interface, implemented in
PluginHost (delegates to McpPluginConfigManager.RefreshPluginConfigsAsync),
and called from PluginController.UpdateSettings after settings save.

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

perf: single-pass XmlReader parser replacing XmlDocument DOM (#86)

* perf: replace XmlDocument with single-pass XmlReader parser

Replace XmlDocument DOM-based parsing with forward-only XmlReader that
produces pre-parsed ParsedSchema DTOs in a single pass. This eliminates
all XPath queries (SelectNodes), repeated attribute access, and DOM
materialization — which profiling showed consumed ~50% of generator time.

Key changes:
- Add SchemaReader: forward-only XmlReader parser producing ParsedSchema
- Add ParsedSchema: immutable DTO record with Types, Composites, Enums,
  Sets, Messages and root metadata (ByteOrder, Package, Version, etc.)
- Change ICodeGenerator interface from XmlDocument to ParsedSchema
- Refactor all generators to iterate pre-parsed DTOs
- Replace LINQ chains in MessagesCodeGenerator with foreach loops
- Remove dead SchemaParser.cs (superseded by SchemaReader)
- Update profiling app to use new ParsedSchema API
- Add InternalsVisibleTo for profiling project

Profiling results (500 iterations, 5 schemas):
  Before: 2.500 ms/iter | After: 1.193 ms/iter (~52% faster)
  XML Parse: 0.700→0.406ms | Types: 0.760→0.221ms | Messages: 0.640→0.398ms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Cache Groups.Cast<GroupDefinition>() and Datas.Cast<DataFieldDefinition>()
  in lazy-initialized typed lists, replacing 24 repeated Cast enumerations
- Remove redundant offset recalculation in AppendFieldsFileContent (offsets
  already set by SumFieldLength in AppendMessageDefinitionConstants)
- Pre-size StringBuilder: 2048+256/field+1024/group+512/data for messages,
  1024 for types/enums/composites/sets
- Replace .Any() with .Count > 0 / HasVariableData property

Profiling (avg of 3 runs, 500 iter × 5 schemas):
  Before: 1.172ms/iter | After: 1.026ms/iter (~12% faster)
  Messages Gen: 0.378→0.359ms | Types Gen: 0.216→0.201ms

* perf: replace string interpolation with chained Append in code generation

Replace ~150 sb.AppendLine($"...{var}...") calls across all generators
and definition builders with chained sb.AppendTabs(tabs).Append().Append()
.AppendLine() calls. This eliminates intermediate string allocations from
interpolation, reducing GC pressure during code generation.

Added AppendTabs(int) fluent helper to StringBuilderExtensions for
tab-prefixed chained append patterns.

Files changed: MessageDefinition, GroupDefinition, CompositeDefinition,
EnumDefinition, EnumFlagsDefinition, TypeDefinition, all Field definitions,
ValidationGenerator, and semantic type definitions.

commit.message:
Rewrite spec.org to align with corrected architecture

Major changes:
- Remove invented concepts: directed program graphs, IR, MSBuild targets
  for graph validation, three-tier binding, hot-swap AssemblyLoadContext,
  NuGet artifact packaging as core arch, CLI as core arch, 7+ diagnostics
- Add separate TInput/TOutput design: plain C# record inputs, [LmpSignature]
  partial record outputs, Predictor<TIn,TOut> binds them
- Add DSPy-grounded modules: ChainOfThought, BestOfN, Refine, ReActAgent,
  ProgramOfThought (post-MVP), BootstrapFewShot, BootstrapRandomSearch,
  MIPROv2 (post-MVP), Evaluator
- Add actual prompt format: source-gen PromptBuilder<TIn,TOut> creates
  ChatMessage[] with [[ ## field ## ]] delimiters, GetResponseAsync<TOut>
- Update platform primitives: .NET 10 / C# 14 / Aspire GA, IChatClient only
- Move ML.NET, Infer.NET, Z3 to post-MVP extensions table
- Reframe from framework to building blocks that compile
- Source gen focus: PromptBuilder, JsonTypeInfo, GetPredictors, CoT extended
  output record, 2-3 diagnostics (LMP001-LMP003)
- Keep all [1]-[11] business case citations with URLs
- Keep platform primitive map (Section 2A), updated for new architecture
- Document intentionally excluded concepts (Section 22)

Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

commit.message:
Rewrite source generator spec to focus on emitted artifacts

Refocus the spec on the five things the generator actually emits:

1. PromptBuilder<TInput, TOutput> — assembles ChatMessage[] from
   instructions, input/output field metadata, demos, and current input.
   Shows the actual prompt format (system, demo pairs, current input).

2. JsonTypeInfo<TOutput> — source-generated JSON serialization context
   for zero-reflection AOT-safe serialization.

3. GetPredictors() on LmpModule — returns all Predictor<,> fields
   without runtime reflection (replaces DSPy named_predictors()).

4. ChainOfThought extended output — internal record adding Reasoning
   field to TOutput when ChainOfThought<TIn,TOut> is used.

5. Diagnostics (LMP001-LMP003 only):
   - LMP001: Output property missing [Description]
   - LMP002: Non-JSON-serializable output property
   - LMP003: [LmpSignature] on non-partial record

Dropped: graph IR, program/step/edge descriptors, binding generators,
MSBuild tasks, DI registration helpers, 7-diagnostic catalog.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Revise PRD to align with v2 system architecture

- Reframe from 'framework' to 'building blocks that compile'
- Add '.NET Compile-Time Advantage' section (source gen vs Python)
- Update to .NET 10 / C# 14 throughout
- Replace directed program graphs with LmpModule + ForwardAsync()

commit.message:
Simplify artifact format spec to match DSPy save/load

Replace the over-engineered artifact format (CompiledArtifact, XxHash128,
SHA-256, AssemblyLoadContext hot-swap, NuGet packaging, polymorphic
StepParameter types, ZIP archives) with a flat JSON format that mirrors
DSPy's module.save()/module.load() pattern.

New format: {version, module, predictors: {name: {instructions, demos,
config}}} — one entry per predictor with instructions, few-shot demos,
and optional LM config. SaveAsync/LoadAsync live directly on LmpModule.

Dropped: variantId, baseProgramHash, validationMetrics, approved,
provenance, bindingMetadata, ICompiledArtifactLoader interface,
FileSystemArtifactLoader, HotSwapArtifactProvider,
CollectibleArtifactContext, ArtifactHashing, .lmpa bundle format.

Post-MVP extensions (NuGet packaging, version hashing, hot reload,
metrics sidecar) are noted briefly but not designed in detail.

commit.message:
docs: rewrite system-architecture.md from first principles

Replace the over-engineered architecture (IR layers, program graphs,
MSBuild targets, three-tier binding) with a simpler 4-layer design:

- Layer 0: .NET Platform (IChatClient, M.E.AI — zero LMP code)
- Layer 1: Core Building Blocks (Predictor<TIn,TOut>, LmpModule, source gen)
- Layer 2: Reasoning Strategies (ChainOfThought, BestOfN, ReAct — thin wrappers)
- Layer 3: Optimization (BootstrapFewShot, Evaluator, IOptimizer)

Key changes: separate TInput/TOutput types, source generators as the
primary .NET advantage, building blocks not a framework.

Add TARGET_APPLE to System.Private.CoreLib DefineConstants for Apple platforms (#126652)

`TypeLoadException: Could not load type
'System.Runtime.InteropServices.Swift.SwiftSelf' from assembly
'System.Private.CoreLib'` on tvos_arm64 CoreCLR. The Swift interop types
(`SwiftSelf`, `SwiftError`, `SwiftIndirectResult`) were being trimmed
from `System.Private.CoreLib.dll` by ILLink on Apple builds.

The `CreateRuntimeRootILLinkDescriptorFile` task generates the ILLink
root descriptor by processing `corelib.h` with
`DefineConstants="$(DefineConstants);FOR_ILLINK"`. Since `corelib.h`
gates the Swift type definitions behind `#ifdef TARGET_APPLE`, and
`TARGET_APPLE` was absent from the C# `DefineConstants`, the task didn't
emit preservation roots for these types. ILLink then trimmed them as
unreachable, causing `CoreLibBinder::GetClass(CLASS__SWIFT_SELF)` to
throw at runtime.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/126557

---------

Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.
